description: The /admin CRM dashboard page: loads entry counts for every content collection and composes the admin shell (summary, count grid, sign-in guide), linking into the Sveltia editor at /admin/editor/.
name: AdminDashboard

description: Production-only client-side passcode gate mounted first in AdminLayout. Renders no visual output — emits a single is:inline script that, on the live site, checks a sessionStorage unlock flag, then shows a custom opaque full-viewport overlay with a masked <input type=password> (built via document.createElement) so the typed passcode renders as dots; reveals the page on a correct entry and redirects to the public home on cancel, Escape, or an incorrect entry. Inert in dev/preview. Its overlay look mirrors AdminGatePrompt.astro and its pure comparison logic is extracted and unit-tested as isPasscodeCorrect in src/lib/adminGate.ts.
name: AdminGate

untestabilityReason.justification: No rendered DOM surface; emits an inline browser script that builds a masked overlay via document.createElement and uses sessionStorage/location glue. Comparison logic tested via isPasscodeCorrect in adminGate.ts; overlay look covered by the AdminGatePrompt scenario.

description: Server-rendered visual twin of the /admin passcode overlay: an on-brand card with a label, a masked <input type=password> (a pre-filled value renders as dots), and Cancel / Unlock actions. The real gate (AdminGate.astro) is production-only and builds its overlay imperatively, so this isolable component is the demonstrable spec of the prompt; the two are kept in sync by hand. Optional `value` prop pre-fills the field to show the masking.
name: AdminGatePrompt

description: A single Board of Directors member card: a circular B&W headshot, or an initials-avatar fallback when the member has no photo, plus name and role. Shared by the landing BoardOfDirectors section and isolated scenarios.
name: BoardMemberCard

description: The 'Connect With Us' social footer from the live chapter pages: a centered heading, the contact email, and the social links rendered as dark circular icon badges. Reads the editable socials + contact email from the settings singleton and reuses socialIconSrc + buildMailto.
name: ChapterConnect

description: Full-bleed city header for a chapter page (mirrors the live harvardintech.com pages): a background photo with a dark overlay and centered light 'HARVARD IN TECH <CITY>' title plus tagline. Falls back to the centered ChapterHeader when heroImage is absent.
name: ChapterHero

description: A single regional chapter page recomposed to match the live harvardintech.com chapter pages: a full-bleed ChapterHero, a ChapterSignUp band, the narrow content block (back link + ChapterLeads + markdown body slot + ChapterLinks), an optional EventGallery (showGallery), and a ChapterConnect social footer. Reused by the /chapters/[slug] route and isolated scenarios.
name: ChapterPage

description: Entry-point page for /chapters/<slug>. Enumerates the chapters content collection via getStaticPaths and renders each chapter through ChapterPage inside BaseLayout.
name: ChapterRoute

description: The teal 'Sign Up' band from the live chapter pages: a flanking-rule 'Sign Up' heading, a 'Join the Harvard in Tech Community' line, and a Subscribe pill defaulting to the shared MailChimp signup.
name: ChapterSignUp

description: A single /admin dashboard card: a content collection entry count, its label, a No-entries-yet note when empty, and a link into the Sveltia editor for that collection.
name: CollectionCountCard

description: The /admin dashboard top row: the heading, a one-line content summary (populated counts or the empty-state nudge), and the primary Open content editor action.
name: DashboardSummary

description: External-form embed for the static site: renders one of three states — a MailChimp-style POST form (action prop), an embedded provider iframe (embedUrl prop), or an unconfigured fallback message. Delegates submission to a third-party service since a static build has no backend.
name: EmbedForm

description: Full Events page body: intro header plus Upcoming and Past sections, split from a flat events array via splitEvents. Reused by the /events route and isolated scenarios.
name: EventsPage

description: Entry-point page for /events. Loads the events content collection and renders the full Events page (Upcoming + Past) via EventsPage inside BaseLayout.
name: EventsRoute

description: Template React island: a name greeting with an interactive click counter. Not used on the Harvard in Tech landing page; retained from the scaffold.
name: Greeting

description: Harvard in Tech public home page — a faithful reproduction of harvardintech.com composed of section components (Hero, WhatsApp Community, Upcoming Events, Get Involved, Board of Directors, Support Us, Event Gallery, Contact Us), reading the events/team content collections and settings/nav singletons.
name: LandingPage

description: Builds a mailto: URL with optional pre-filled subject/body, normalizing space encoding to %20. Used by the Support Us, Contact Us, and footer links.
name: buildMailto

description: Returns the directory the content collections load from: the CODEYAM_CONTENT_ROOT sandbox during a codeyam session, else the committed src/content.
name: contentRoot

description: Builds the /admin dashboard summary sentence: an empty-state nudge at zero, otherwise the total entries and collection count with correct singular/plural grammar.
name: dashboardSummaryText

description: Sums entry counts across the dashboard collection cards and reports how many collections there are, for the /admin summary line.
name: dashboardTotals

description: Returns the directory the JSON singletons (settings, nav) load from: the CODEYAM_DATA_ROOT sandbox during a codeyam session, else the committed src/data.
name: dataRoot

description: Returns a new array of board members visible on the public Board of Directors, keeping every member whose active field is not explicitly false — so existing entries with no active field (and members who never toggled the CMS checkbox) remain shown. Does not mutate the input.
name: filterActiveBoardMembers

description: Formats an event date as a long en-US date string (e.g. 'September 24, 2026') for display on event cards.
name: formatEventDate

description: Pure decision for the Event Gallery fade-in: returns true when every tile should be shown immediately instead of animated — when the user prefers reduced motion, or the browser lacks IntersectionObserver (old browsers / SSR) so tiles never get stuck invisible.
name: galleryRevealImmediately

description: Returns the per-tile CSS transition-delay (ms) that staggers the Event Gallery fade-in across grid columns; tiles in the same column share a delay so each column ripples in together. Defaults to 5 columns, 60ms per column step.
name: galleryStaggerDelay

description: Dev-only helper that (re)creates the .codeyam/tmp/content-sandbox copy of src/content and src/data, so the codeyam dev server reads/seeds the sandbox and never the committed production markdown.
name: initContentSandbox

untestabilityReason.justification: filesystem boundary — rmSync/cpSync of content/data dirs; no branching logic to unit-test

description: Idempotent, SSR-safe DOM wiring for the Event Gallery fade-in: reveals every tile immediately when galleryRevealImmediately says to, otherwise observes each tile via IntersectionObserver and adds the in-view class as it scrolls into the viewport, unobserving once revealed.
name: initGalleryReveal

description: Idempotent client-side DOM wiring for the banner parallax: no-op under SSR/vitest and on re-invocation. When parallaxEnabled (read via matchMedia) is true, sizes every [data-parallax] to 100vh and, on a rAF-throttled scroll/resize listener, sets translateY(parallaxOffset(rootTop)) so the photo stays fixed while its [data-parallax-root] section scrolls over it; otherwise leaves the images as static object-fit: cover. Hoisted from Hero.astro and GetInvolved.astro.
name: initParallax

description: Derives up-to-two-letter uppercase initials from a board member name, used as the BoardMemberCard avatar fallback when a member has no photo.
name: initials

description: Pure passcode comparison for the /admin dashboard deterrent gate: trims input and expected, treats an empty expected value as never-matching, and returns whether they are equal. The inline gate script in AdminGate.astro mirrors this logic.
name: isPasscodeCorrect

description: Pure gate deciding whether the scroll parallax runs: returns fineHover && !reducedMotion. Encodes the desktop-only / prefers-reduced-motion policy (parallax is janky or ignored on touch browsers) as testable logic, separate from the matchMedia DOM reads in initParallax.
name: parallaxEnabled

description: Pure helper returning the translateY (px) that keeps a banner pinned to the viewport top as its section scrolls: returns -sectionTop, where sectionTop is the section's getBoundingClientRect().top. Reproduces the background-attachment: fixed look on the Hero and Get Involved banners without CSS background-image (which the preview proxy / Pages base path rewrite breaks).
name: parallaxOffset

description: Reads and JSON-parses a singleton data file (settings.json / nav.json) from the resolved dataRoot(), so the site settings/nav honor the codeyam sandbox redirect.
name: readSingleton

untestabilityReason.justification: filesystem boundary — readFileSync + JSON.parse of a data-dir file

description: Maps a social link icon field (twitter/facebook/email, case-insensitive) to its self-hosted asset path under public/images/social/. Returns null for unknown or missing icons so the Contact Us section degrades to a label-only link. Icons were downloaded from the original harvardintech.com site to keep the GitHub Pages build CDN-independent.
name: socialIconSrc

description: Returns a new array of board members ordered by ascending order field (missing order treated as 0), so the public Board of Directors matches the curated sequence regardless of on-disk file order.
name: sortBoardMembers

description: Splits a flat event list into upcoming (date >= now, soonest first) and past (date < now, most recent first) relative to a reference time; events at now count as upcoming.
name: splitEvents

description: Splits a board member's role into display lines on a '·' separator (e.g. "Founder · Harvard C'08" -> ["Founder", "Harvard C'08"]), trimming surrounding whitespace; a role without a separator returns a single-element array. Used by BoardMemberCard to render the role over two lines.
name: splitRole

description: Coerces a string-or-Date event date into a Date, passing existing Date instances through untouched.
name: toEventDate

description: Prefixes an internal absolute path with import.meta.env.BASE_URL so links resolve under either the domain root (custom domain, base /) or a project subpath (user.github.io/<repo>). External URLs, mailto, and bare hash anchors pass through untouched. Used by the header nav/logo, chapter back link, 404, and blog links for GitHub Pages deployment.
name: withBase
